data.title: Look Past Oil. This Commodity Could Face Huge Market Fallout From the Iran War. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Alex Kozul-Wright

The war in Iran poses a major threat to aluminum supplies. Used in everything from cars to dishwashers, aluminum was already in a structural deficit before the conflict began and a further shortage could have big knock-on effects.

The Middle East accounts for 9% of global aluminum production and meets 18% of world demand, excluding China, according to ANZ. The bank says four-to-five million metric tons of exports are at risk.

"The Middle East has emerged over the past decade as a pivotal production and export hub for aluminum," analyst Soni Kumari said in a research note published Thursday. "The longer the Strait of Hormuz stays closed, the greater the impact [on prices]", she added.

Aluminum forward contracts were up 1.1% early Thursday at $3,181.75 per metric ton in New York. Prices are up 4.5% since Feb. 27, the day before the Iran conflict began.

Alba — a leading aluminum producer in Bahrain — declared force majeure, freeing the company from its contractual liabilities, on March 4 due to the conflict, according to multiple sources.

Smelters take time to return to full capacity after closures. As global aluminum prices rise, that should eventually feed through into downstream products.

In the near-term, executives at car parts companies told the Financial Times that car makers were trying to build up contingency inventories amid continued fighting in the Middle East.

Traders in Japan were also reported as saying that the country's auto companies were thinking about securing supplies from Russia, having halted purchases from Russian suppliers since the invasion of Ukraine in 2022.

Asian companies are at the sharp end of disruptions. The price of everything from Korean electronic goods to Indian beer cans may also get a jolt.

The Dow Jones U.S. Aluminum Index closed 3.6% higher on Wednesday. However, industrial mining companies have fared poorly since fighting erupted, on Feb. 28.

Alcoa is down 7.5% over the past month, while Century Aluminum has fallen 3.9% over the same period, possibly on the back of higher energy prices — a key cost for miners — linked to the war.

Write to Alex Kozul-Wright at alexander.kozul-wright@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Ocean Power Technologies ships first PowerBuoy under $6.5M DHS contract
data.description:
Ocean Power Technologies announced shipment of the first PowerBuoy system under its $6.5M contract with the U.S. Department of Homeland Security.

Key Highlights:

  • Shipped first PowerBuoy system under the previously announced $6.5M DHS contract to support U.S. Coast Guard operations.
  • Shipment marks transition from development to operational deployment for persistent offshore surveillance.
  • Systems will be deployed off California to provide autonomous maritime domain awareness for DHS mission needs.
  • Integration with Anduril’s systems is progressing in parallel, supporting networked surveillance capabilities.
  • Deployment pending final site preparations and operational readiness before supporting DHS maritime surveillance.

Original SEC Filing: Ocean Power Technologies, Inc. [ OPTT ] - 8-K - Mar. 26, 2026

data.title: Dogwood posts shareholder letter: 145 patients enrolled in Halneuron Phase 2b; $12.5M raised
data.description:
Dogwood reported 145 patients enrolled in its Halneuron Phase 2b study and raised $12.5 million to fund operations through data release this fall.

Key Highlights:

  • Recruited 145 patients in Halneuron Phase 2b HALT-CINP; final study data projected for release this fall.
  • Raised $12.5 million this quarter; combined with 2025 year-end cash provides runway through Halneuron data release.
  • Filed IND for SP16 IV under a royalty-free global development and commercialization license to treat cancer-related pain and neuropathy.
  • SP16 Phase 1b trial fully funded by a National Cancer Institute development grant; dosing expected mid-2026.
  • Advancing synthesized Halneuron formulation for Phase 3 to lower manufacturing costs and improve yields.

Original SEC Filing: Dogwood Therapeutics, Inc. [ DWTX ] - 8-K - Mar. 26, 2026

data.title: Super Micro Faces a Securities Fraud Lawsuit. The Stock Is Falling. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Nate Wolf

Super Micro Computer stock fell Thursday after an investor filed a class action lawsuit against the chip maker and its two top executives in federal court, alleging securities fraud related to last week's chip-smuggling indictments.

The complaint, filed in the U.S. District Court for Northern California, accuses the company of failing to disclose sales of servers to companies based in China in violation of U.S. export-control laws. CEO Charles Liang and Chief Financial Officer David Weigand are named as co-defendants in the suit.

Shareholders saw the stock plunge 33% last Friday after the Justice Department charged a co-founder of the company and two other individuals over an alleged plan to divert U.S.-assembled servers to China without a license. The company itself was not named as a defendant.

Super Micro didn't immediately respond to Barron's request for comment Thursday.

"Super Micro maintains a robust compliance program and is committed to full adherence to all applicable U.S. export and re-export control laws and regulations," the company said in a statement after last week's indictments. "The Company has been cooperating fully with the government's investigation and will continue to do so."

Shares were down 3.1% to $23.31 on Thursday. The stock has fallen 18% this year and 31% over the last 12 months as of Wednesday's close.

Law firms will now try to recruit a class of litigants who lost money on their Super Micro shares to join the federal lawsuit. Shareholders have 60 days to file a motion to be considered as a lead plaintiff.

Investigations for potential class action suits are common when a listed company's share price tumbles, with entire legal practices devoted to similar types of securities fraud cases. This civil suit adds to Super Micro's headache as the company continues to manage the fallout of last week's criminal charges.

Super Micro has withstood controversy in the past. The stock was delisted from the exchange in August 2018 for delaying the filing of financial reports. In 2024, a short seller raised concerns about the company's accounting practices and its accounting firm Ernst & Young later resigned. Super Micro said the short-report contained "false or inaccurate statements," including "misleading presentations of information."

The company hasn't lost market share in the server business despite a decade of accounting and compliance concerns, Susquehanna analyst Mehdi Hosseini told Barron's last week. He doesn't expect a mass exodus of customers this time around, either.

Write to Nate Wolf at nate.wolf@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: AFRICA-FX- Ghana's cedi seen weakening, Zambia's kwacha stable, traders say
data.description:
Ghana's cedi is expected to weaken against the dollar in the coming week to Thursday, while Zambia's kwacha is likely to remain stable, traders said on Thursday.

GHANA

The cedi could face further pressure next week as dollar demand, predominantly from the energy, manufacturing, and commerce sectors, continues to outweigh supply in the interbank market.

London Stock Exchange Group data showed the cedi trading at 10.93 to the dollar on Thursday, compared to 10.90 a week earlier.

"The cedi continues to face modest depreciation pressure as demand persistently outpace supply, with the Bank of Ghana's FX intermediation auction providing only a partial offset," said Ronald Mensah, a trader at Stanbic Bank Ghana."

"Barring a meaningful shift in liquidity conditions, the currency's relative weakness looks set to extend into the near term," he added.

Andrews Akoto, head of trading at Absa Bank Ghana, echoed similar sentiments, noting that FX liquidity conditions will likely remain tight in the coming week.

ZAMBIA

The kwacha is likely to hold steady in the near term, helped by improved liquidity conditions and increased hard currency sales ahead of end-month obligations.

On Thursday, commercial banks quoted the currency at 19.02 per dollar, strengthening from 19.75 a week earlier.

Access Bank (ACCESS.GH) said in a note that liquidity conditions have improved, likely due to anticipated foreign exchange inflows in preparation for a government bond auction.

"These factors could continue to support the kwacha in the near-term," Access Bank said.

UGANDA

The Ugandan shilling is expected to trade with a weakening bias in the coming days, pressured by end-month hard currency demand from merchandise importers, traders said on Thursday.

At 0823 GMT, commercial banks quoted the shilling at 3,712/3,722 per dollar, compared with last Thursday's close of 3,775/3,785.

The market is receiving customary end-of-month demand for dollars from merchandise importers in energy, manufacturing and other sector, said an independent foreign exchange trader in the capital Kampala.

"I think against that demand we'll see the shilling remain under moderate pressure," the trader said.

KENYA

The Kenyan shilling is likely to remain stable in the coming days, supported by limited appetite for hard currency.

On Thursday, the shilling traded at 129.70/90 per dollar, slightly weaker than last Thursday's close of 129.40/60.

data.title: India asks auto industry to optimise production as Iran war hurts energy supplies
data.description:
By Aditi Shah

India has asked automakers and parts suppliers to tighten production schedules to conserve fuel amid fears of shortages caused by disrupted oil and gas imports from the Gulf due to the Iran war, a government memo seen by Reuters shows.

The heavy industries ministry has also urged companies to shift factory operations from oil-based fuels to electricity and to use recycled aluminium or alternative materials as shortages and costs rise, according to the March 25 advisory.

For India, one of the world's largest oil and gas importers, the advisory underscores the government's mounting concern over the conflict and its disruption to energy flows, supply chains and availability of raw materials.

India's ministry of heavy industries did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

The government has already prioritised use of gas for households over industries, which get only about 80% of their average needs.

Some parts suppliers to India's leading carmakers like Maruti Suzuki , Tata Motors and Mahindra are already reporting a shortage of gas to power operations at a time when vehicle sales are booming.

The ministry wants the sector to do more.

"Wherever technically feasible, a transition from oil-based fuels to electricity may be considered. Further, production schedules may be optimised to minimise idle and standby fuel consumption," the ministry said in its note.

The government wants companies to use recycled aluminium where possible and explore the use of alternative materials for packaging and other non-critical applications to reduce "demand pressure" amid shortages which are already affecting beer makers.

"I don't know how much we can change in the factory, but the takeaway is that this war is going to go on for a long time and we should be prepared," said an executive at an Indian carmaker.

data.title: Accenture Is Speeding Up Cybersecurity With AI
data.description:
Accenture is trying to rethink how cybersecurity actually works, rolling out a new AI driven platform called Cyber.AI that is built to move much faster than traditional systems.

At the center of it is Anthropic's Claude model, which acts as the brain behind the platform. It pulls together security data, makes sense of it, and helps respond in real time. The goal is to move away from the usual stop and start approach, where systems are checked periodically, and toward something that is always running in the background, constantly scanning and reacting.

Accenture has already been using it internally, and the results are pretty telling. The platform is now helping secure around 1,600 applications and more than 500,000 APIs. What really stands out is the speed. Tasks that used to take 3 to 5 days are now being done in under 1 hour. At the same time, coverage has jumped from about 10% to over 80%, and service delivery has improved by 35%, which is also helping bring costs down over time.

data.title: Marchex 2025 10-K: Revenue $45.4M, GAAP EPS $(0.12)
data.description:
Marchex reported fiscal 2025 revenue of $45.4 million, down 6% year-over-year primarily due to lower call volumes and contract consolidations, and delivered a net loss per share of $(0.12). The company recorded an operating loss of $(5.7) million, which drove a net loss attributable to common stockholders of $(5.2) million, partially offset by other income. Marchex continues to align costs through reorganization and capitalization of certain software development expenditures while noting potential future financing needs.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $45.4 million, decreased 6% year-over-year driven by lower call volumes and contract consolidation.
  • Operating Income (Loss from operations): $(5.7) million (loss), representing -13% of revenue, widened from $(4.4) million in the prior year.
  • Net Income (Net loss applicable to common stockholders): $(5.2) million, impacted by the operating loss and partially offset by other income.
  • Net Income Per Share (Basic and diluted net loss per Class A and B share applicable to common stockholders): $(0.12).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Trend: Revenue declined 6% to $45.4 million in 2025, attributed to lower call volumes and consolidation of contracts.
  • Channel Strategy: Continued emphasis on phone, text and multi-channel conversational data with revenue recognized over time as services are performed.
  • Operational Efficiency: Reorganization actions reduced product development and cloud costs; some development work has been shifted to capitalized software to align expenses with longer-term asset recognition.
  • Market Position: Focus on AI-powered vertical market conversational intelligence targeted at large B2B2C enterprises.
  • Milestones & Risks: Completed intangible amortization and capitalized software development; management noted the potential need for additional financing moving forward.

Original SEC Filing: MARCHEX INC [ MCHX ] - 10-K - Mar. 26, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Dell Director Offloads Hundreds of Thousands of Shares as Insider Selling Tops $330M
data.description:
A director linked to Dell Technologies sold hundreds of thousands of company shares in recent transactions, part of a series of insider stock sales disclosed this week.

The filing showed that V (GP) L.L.C. Slta disposed of 459,999 shares on March 23 at an average price of $162.25, for proceeds of about $74.6 million. The same entity carried out several additional sales earlier in March, bringing the total value of stock sold in those trades to roughly $336 million.

The activity comes weeks after Dell reported quarterly earnings that exceeded analysts' estimates. The technology company posted earnings of $3.89 per share on revenue of $33.38 billion, compared with market expectations of $3.53 per share and $31.6 billion in revenue. Sales increased nearly 40% from the same period a year earlier.

Dell also raised its quarterly dividend to $0.63 per share from $0.53, with the payment scheduled for May 1 for shareholders on record as of April 21.

Shares of Dell climbed to around $176 in recent trading and are approaching a one-year high near $178.
